## reference · dashboard data density glanceability preattentive drill-down information design at-a-glance KPI
* Dashboards are collections of data visualizations, presented in a single-page view that imparts at-a-glance information on which users can act quickly.
* Operational dashboards aim to impart critical information quickly to users as they are engaged in time-sensitive tasks.
* Analytical dashboards provide the user with at-a-glance information used for analysis and decision making, but don’t have the same level of time sensitivity as operational dashboards.
* Leverage preattentive processing attributes such as length, 2D position, and color to communicate quantitative information quickly.
* Use length and 2D position for quantitative representation, as they are ideal for quantitative comparison.
* Avoid using color to communicate information about quantitative values or magnitude.
* Bar charts and line graphs are effective for representing quantitative data, as they leverage preattentive attributes like length and 2D position.
* Circular graphs like pie charts, gauges, and radar charts are difficult to interpret quickly or accurately, as they rely on area and angle to communicate quantitative information.
* Tree maps can be useful for representing complex data sets, but are not suitable for simple, actionable dashboards.
* Avoid using pie charts, donut charts, and gauges, as they are poor at communicating complex quantitative relationships among items in a data set.
* Use linear bullet charts instead of radial gauges to communicate quantitative data along a range with a target value identified.
Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/dashboards-preattentive/
